content.boundary: Applies to resource allocation, curriculum support, and school-quality interventions for comprehensive-track and feeder schools; explicitly leaves the 6/8-year gimnázium structure, admissions mechanism, and selection age untouched
content.change_lever: Introduce targeted, needs-based supplementary funding and quality-levelling programmes for schools that lose high-performing students to the selective track, modeled on evidence-linked compensatory approaches, without touching admission criteria, track length, or selection age
content.name: Non-structural compensatory equity package (Portugal-style, selection age unchanged)
content.system_problem: Only 12.1% of disadvantaged students reach a good level in at least one basic skill (versus 16.3% EU average), disadvantaged schools show systematically higher rates of unqualified teachers, and per-capita financing means feeder schools lose both funding and positive peer effects when high performers depart for gimnázium, with no compensatory mechanism
